suggestions.title: Desk assignment non-deterministic — workers teleport between polling cycles
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1311-1323: deskOccupants fills slots by iteration order (working first, then idle, then ghosts). If any worker's status changes between polling cycles, ALL subsequent workers get reassigned to different desks. Carlos leaves a task → Dave slides left → Maya jumps to Dave's old seat. The 'office' metaphor breaks because a worker's desk identity resets every 30 seconds. Fix: assign each worker a stable desk slot by hashing their id against the DESKS array (e.g., DESKS[stableHash(worker.id) % DESKS.length]), falling back to first-available only when that slot is already taken.

suggestions.title: R-key refresh bypasses onResume — PAUSED banner persists (strike 3)
suggestions.description: App.jsx keyboard shortcut handler (inside the useEffect at line ~896): calls syncFromGitHub() directly on R/r keypress instead of onResume(). onResume() is the correct path — it sets pollingPaused(false) and updates lastActivity.current. Without it, pressing R while paused fires one fetch but the PAUSED banner remains visible, and the 15-second inactivity check re-parks polling immediately on the next tick. One-line fix: replace syncFromGitHub() with onResume() in the keydown handler. This has been flagged three reviews in a row.

suggestions.title: Activity log display order depends on unsorted API response
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 140: `const displayed = entries.slice(0, 20).reverse()` — no sort by timestamp before slicing. The code assumes activity.json is always in reverse-chronological order (newest entry at index 0). If the API ever returns entries out-of-order, or if synthetic vibe-transition entries (prepended at line 1111) interleave incorrectly with fetched entries, the time-group buckets ('Just now', 'Earlier today') will be populated with wrong entries and the chronological display breaks. Fix: sort entries by timestamp descending before slicing — `[...entries].sort((a, b) => new Date(b.timestamp) - new Date(a.timestamp)).slice(0, 20).reverse()`.

suggestions.title: Single error worker triggers team-wide alarm state
suggestions.description: vibe.js line 8-9: `workers.some(w => w.status === 'error')` — one error in a 5-person team puts the entire vibe into '🚨 On Fire', floods the header with the red border, changes Bloberto's expression, and triggers the stat-card error pulse. If a worker stays in error state for hours (common), this creates persistent alarm that drowns out actual emergencies. Consider a proportional threshold: error vibe only if >20% of workers are in error, or if the error has persisted >30 minutes. A single transient error should show the error stat-card without escalating the whole team vibe.

suggestions.title: Workers transitioning to 'done' status get no celebration (strike 3)
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 964: activeWorkers filters on status === 'working' | 'idle' | 'error'. Workers that go working→done are excluded from activeWorkers and fall into the 'disappeared' branch (400ms fade + door depart event). The justShipped check at line 1037 only fires for working→idle. The entire WORKER_COMPLETION_LINES map is wasted for done-status workers. Fix: in the diff effect, separately detect working→done transitions and trigger the same confetti+toast+lines flow used for working→idle.

suggestions.title: Speech bubble overflows office floor border on mobile (strike 2)
suggestions.description: Confirmed in screenshot qa-sofia-mobile.png: Bloberto's speech bubble ('my customer says we are aligned.') extends above the office-floor's cyan border and overlaps the tab navigation bar. The bubble uses `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` relative to the character element with z-index: 30. When Bloberto is positioned near top: 4% of the floor (Office.jsx line ~1578), the bubble escapes the container. The floor uses `overflow: clip` with `overflow-clip-margin: 20px` — not enough clearance for the bubble height. Fix: add `clamp()` on the bubble's bottom offset on small viewports, or set `max(bottom, 4px)` relative to the floor top edge.

suggestions.title: Mobile: pinged hover card has no visible dismiss control (strike 3)
suggestions.description: On touch devices, tapping a character opens a hover card that can only be dismissed by tapping elsewhere on the floor. There is no close button rendered inside the card. Touch users have no visible affordance for dismissal — especially problematic on mobile where accidental mis-taps on adjacent desk elements are common. Add a small ✕ button (or swipe-down gesture) visible only when `pinnedCard === worker.id`, rendered inside the character hover card's bottom-right corner.

suggestions.title: Character tooltips use CSS data-tooltip attribute, not screen reader accessible
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 744-745: character tooltip text is set via `data-tooltip` attribute, rendered by Office.css as a CSS `::before` pseudo-element. This pattern is invisible to screen readers — pseudo-element content from CSS is not in the accessibility tree. Workers' task descriptions and status tooltips (the most useful info for AT users) are therefore inaccessible. Fix: add `title` attribute in addition to `data-tooltip`, or replace with an actual aria-describedby pointing to a visually-hidden span. The title attribute is already used on the desk monitor (line 1480) — apply the same approach to Character elements.

suggestions.title: App.jsx has grown to 1300+ lines — still a single monolithic file (strike 3)
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains: ErrorBoundary, ActivityLogEntry, ActivityLog, ProgressBar, StatusBadge, WorkerCard, StatsBar, DeltaBadge, VibeSparkline, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton, ShortcutToast, and the main App component — all in one file with 63KB of source. Suggested split: components/WorkerCard.jsx, components/ActivityLog.jsx, components/StatsBar.jsx, components/ShortcutToast.jsx, components/LoadingSkeleton.jsx, hooks/useWorkerSync.js (the polling logic). This has been flagged three consecutive reviews with no action.

suggestions.title: Footer quote button missing aria-label
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~1469: `<button className='footer-tagline' title='tap for another thought'>` — title attribute is not reliably announced by VoiceOver/NVDA, and is completely silent for non-hover AT users. The button has no visible label text (just a sparkle icon + quote text that changes every click). Add `aria-label='Show another quote'` and update it dynamically to reflect the current quote number (e.g., `aria-label={quoteBonus > 0 ? \`Quote ${quoteBonus} of ${BONUS_TAGLINES.length} — click for next\` : 'Show team quote'}`). Small effort, real accessibility gain.

suggestions.title: Worker cards not keyboard-reachable via Tab (strike 3)
suggestions.description: WorkerCard renders with tabIndex={-1} (App.jsx line 321). Only the ℹ️ button inside each card is reachable via Tab. A keyboard-only user cannot navigate between cards, cannot trigger onTagClick, and has no way to know the worker name/status without tabbing into each card's internal control. The cards have role='article' and aria-label with full worker context — they should be focusable. Change tabIndex to 0 and add a focus style. If the large focus surface is a design concern, at minimum add a visually-hidden 'View [name] details' button as the first focusable child of each card.

suggestions.title: isTouchDevice evaluated at module scope — stale for hybrid devices (strike 3)
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 692: `const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is a module-level constant. On Surface/iPad users who attach a keyboard mid-session, this stays false for the entire session. The PAUSED pill copy ('Tap to refresh' vs 'Move mouse to refresh') becomes wrong. Character.jsx already does this correctly by reading the media query inside the event handler. Move the isTouchDevice read into the relevant render/callback path or use a hook that subscribes to mediaquery changes.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary fallback uses hardcoded dark-mode colors (strike 3)
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 17-20: `color: '#f87171'` is hardcoded in the ErrorBoundary render — not using CSS custom properties. In light mode, this renders low-contrast red text on a light background. The Retry button has no styling at all. Use `var(--error)` for the color and apply `className='btn btn-sm'` to the button. Two-line fix with no risk.

suggestions.title: Stats bar renders up to 7 cards with no overflow handling at mid-range widths
suggestions.description: StatsBar renders: On the Clock, Active, Idle, Sync Status, Team Vibe, and conditionally Error + Stuck. At 7 visible cards, the flex-wrap layout at ~768px creates awkward 2-row layouts with varying heights. The @media (max-width: 900px) breakpoint in App.css reduces font sizes but doesn't cap the number of visible cards or introduce a horizontal scroll. On a 13-inch laptop at ~960px, the stats bar consistently wraps to 2 rows. Consider either a horizontal scroll container (overflow-x: auto, scrollbar hidden) or collapsing error/stuck cards into the primary stat cards as icon badges at medium widths.

suggestions.title: Vibe history pushed on initial mount — first entry is always the current vibe (strike 3)
suggestions.description: App.jsx inside the teamVibe.key useEffect: the isInitialMount guard (previousVibeKeyRef.current === null) protects the streak counter increment but NOT the history push at lines 1113-1119. Every cold page load appends the current vibe to history before any real transition has occurred. After 12 page loads, the sparkline shows 12 identical vibe dots with no real transitions — defeating its entire purpose. Wrap the history push inside `if (!isInitialMount) { ... }` just like the streak logic above it.

verified_fixes: quotes.js now exports VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES — Office.jsx line 6 imports it correctly, no runtime error on vibe transitions
Conference table SVG glow filter now uses useId() (Office.jsx line ~908) — no more hardcoded id collision risk
JSON.parse on VIBE_HISTORY_KEY wrapped in try/catch (App.jsx lines 1114-1115) — corrupted localStorage no longer throws
Full-sync and full-idle banners now have aria-live='polite' — screen readers are informed of state transitions
praise: The desk away-sign (sticky note with worker name and idle duration) is a genuinely clever detail — AwaySign renders after 10 idle minutes with idleMinutes as context, and the CSS sticky-note aesthetic is pixel-perfect
Stale working detection with three tiers (fresh/stale/abandoned) and the worker-duration data-tier coloring is exactly the right level of signal density — not alarming, just informative
The ficus plant lifecycle (seedling → sapling → lush → blooming + grief toast on streak break) is the best easter egg in the app — mechanically sound, emotionally resonant
WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS per-worker ('in the logs', 'sketching', 'headphones on') gives every idle character a personality without adding UI noise
The WallClock minute-hand going frantic during 'on-fire' vibe is a subtle micro-interaction that rewards attention
Conference table meeting detection (2+ workers with 'meeting' tasks) is a smart emergent behavior — no configuration needed, pure data-driven
Polling idle detection (10-min IDLE_TIMEOUT) with PAUSED pill and onResume is the correct UX pattern for a background dashboard — saves API calls and respects the user's attention
The ping feature (click any worker character → personality speech bubble) is completely undocumented and completely delightful — it's the best kind of easter egg
